subject: Human Resources
title: Stock Options Explained
description: A fast breakdown of Stock Options Explained for builders.

hook.text: An early Google employee exercised stock options worth $10M. A late-stage hire's options were worth almost nothing.

buildup.text: Stock options give employees the right to buy company shares at a set price—the 'strike price'—after a vesting period.

discovery.text: If the company's stock rises above the strike price, options become valuable. If it falls below, they're worthless.

twist.text: Most startup options expire worthless—because most startups fail or never go public. The lottery analogy is real.

climax.text: Understanding options requires knowing: strike price, vesting schedule, exercise window, and tax implications.

punchline.text: Options are a bet on the future. Know the odds before you gamble.

quiz.question: When do stock options become valuable?
options: When the stock price rises above the strike price
When the company reduces its stock price
